sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Check what changed between previous and current frame
if last_transition:
    before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    after_seg = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation
    
    before_nodes = {n['hash']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
    after_nodes = {n['hash']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}
    
    print("Before node hashes:", sorted(before_nodes.keys()))
    print("After node hashes:", sorted(after_nodes.keys()))
    
    # Check for any differences
    for h in before_nodes:
        if h in after_nodes:
            bn = before_nodes[h]
            an = after_nodes[h]
            if bn['color'] != an['color'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']:
                print(f"Changed: hash={h}, color {bn['color']}->{an['color']}, pixels {bn['pixels']}->{an['pixels']}")
    
    print("\nBoard changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))
    print("Animation changed:", last_action_result.get('animation_changed'))

# Let me also check the current segmentation more carefully
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("\nCurrent nodes summary:")
for node in nodes:
    if node['pixels'] < 100:
        rows = [p[0] for p in node['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in node['boundary']]
        print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, px={node['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
